content.text:
<session>
This Django view is making hundreds of database queries per request:

def order_dashboard(request):
    orders = Order.objects.filter(status='pending')
    result = []
    for order in orders:
        result.append({
            'id': order.id,
            'customer': order.user.email,
            'items': [
                {
                    'name': item.product.name,
                    'price': item.product.price,
                    'qty': item.quantity
                }
                for item in order.items.all()
            ],
            'total': sum(
                item.product.price * item.quantity
                for item in order.items.all()
            ),
            'shipping': order.shipping_address.city
        })
    return JsonResponse({'orders': result})

The models are:
- Order: has user (FK), shipping_address (FK)
- OrderItem: has order (FK), product (FK), quantity
- Product: has name, price
- ShippingAddress: has city, street, zip

Fix every N+1 query. Show the exact queryset and explain
how many queries it makes before and after.
</session>

Write the title in the language the user wrote in, regardless of the language of the examples above.

system.text: You are Claude Code, Anthropic's official CLI for Claude.

content.text: {"title":"Fix N+1 queries in Django order dashboard"}
